channel.title: Spring
channel.description: Level up your Java code and explore what Spring can do for you.

item.title: A Bootiful Podcast: Russ Miles on Safer, More Productive Interactions with AI
item.dc:creator: joshlong
item.content:encoded: IˇŻm joined, I think, for the second time by my friend, the legendary architect and developer extraordinaire Russ Miles, who has given some of the most cerebral and exciting Spring keynotes and talks ever. And this time, heˇŻs back to talk about ? guess what ? A.I. As usual, heˇŻs got a different and very interesting angle on the whole thing in this short-but-sweet episode. Recorded at UberConf 2026 in Westminster, Colorado!

item.title: This Week in Spring - July 14th, 2026
item.dc:creator: joshlong
item.content:encoded:
This Week in Spring - July 14th, 2026

In two short months, on this date in September, Java 27 will be here! Who's excited? I'm excited!

I'm in delightful Denver, CO, for the amazing UberConf. I just delivered a workshop that was scheduled from 09:00 to 17:00, but it went into overtime, and we just concluded at 18:30. Long day (story of my life)! Such a nice way to spend the day, though. Surrounded by cool people. As usual, though, the Spring ecosystem waits for no one. Let's dive right into this amazing week in Spring!

  • I did a video you might like, and I really hope you'll watch it, looking at what motivates Spring Framework and how we approach building things with it.
  • In last week's installment of A Bootiful Podcast, I talked to Spring Boot teammate Moritz Halbritter.
  • Loic Mathieu has a nice post on some of the amazing new features in Java 27
  • I can't believe I missed this: my friend and architecture extraordinairre Gregor Hohpe has an amazing interview on being an architect
  • Craig Walls is back with an amazing post on applying semantic guardrails
  • And another one by Craig Walls, this one on safeguarding LLM output
  • I love this: Spring AI AgentCore 2.0.0 is available
  • I loved this article on scaling Java-based real-time systems
  • InfoQ had a nice post way back in April on the concepts going beyond RAG in an AI application with Spring Boot

item.title: Spring Office Hours Podcast: S5E18 - The Latest from OpenAI, Anthropic and Spring AI 2.0
item.dc:creator: danvega
item.content:encoded:
Join Dan Vega and DaShaun Carter for the latest updates from the Spring Ecosystem. In this episode, Dan and DaShaun catch up on the fast moving world of AI and what it means for Java developers. The guys will cover recent releases from OpenAI and Anthropic, then dig into Spring AI 2.0, which is now generally available and built for Spring Boot 4 and Spring Framework 7. You will learn about the new unified tool calling model, progressive tool discovery, and what has changed in MCP support. You can participate in our live stream to ask questions or catch the replay on your preferred podcast platform.

Show Notes

  • Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: A next-generation model
  • Redeploying Claude Fable 5
  • Spring AI 2.0 GA Available Now
  • Spring AI Recipes

item.title: A Bootiful Podcast: Spring Boot legend Moritz Halbritter on the latest and greatest in Spring Boot 4 and 4.1
item.dc:creator: joshlong
item.content:encoded: Hi Spring fans! Spring Boot 4.1 is here and with it a ton of amazing features. I'm delighted to have had the opportunity to sit down with Moritz Halbritter, of Spring Boot and Spring Initializr fame, to talk about some of the amazing features.
